Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 087. A Messenger from Borsippa (ABL 1433)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL EN-ía]

(1) [To the king, my lord: your servant Ṭ]ab-ṣill-E[šarra. Good health] to the ki[ng, my lord!] May [Aššur] and Mullissu bless the king, my lord!

o 22

[ARAD-ka mDÙG].⸢GA⸣—ṣil—⸢É⸣.[ŠÁR.RA]

o 33

[lu DI-mu] a-na ⸢LUGAL⸣ [EN-ía]

o 44

[aš-šur d]NIN.LÍL a-⸢na⸣ [LUGAL]

o 55

EN-ia lik-ru-bu

o 66

mṣu-lu-lu LÚ.[A—šip-ri]

(6) Ṣululu, a [messenger] of Sulu[...] the commandant of Borsippa, came to Birat; from Birat, he [was] escorted to Hinzanu by the messenger of the Biratean, and from Hinzanu they came to the Inner City.

o 77

ša msu-lu-⸢x⸣

o 88

LÚv.ši-kín—ṭè-⸢me⸣

o 99

ša URU.bur-si-⸢bi*⸣

a-na KUR.bi-ra-a-te

o 1111

i-tal-ka TA KUR.bi-ra-[a-te]

o 1212

LÚv.A—šip-ri ša KUR.bi-rat-⸢a*⸣.[a]

i-si-šú a-na URU.ḫe-en-⸢za⸣-[na i-tal-ka]

o 1414

(16) [......] with my m[esse]nger ......

(Rest destroyed)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334904/.
